title: Hieracium caloprasinum
taxonomy: Hieracium caloprasinum is a member of the dandelion-like flowering plants known as hawkweeds, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is further classified within the genus Hieracium, which comprises over 1000 species of flowering plants. The genus Hieracium is a part of the tribe Senecioneae, a group of plants characterized by their daisy-like flowers and often bitter or milky sap. Hieracium caloprasinum is thought to be native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia, where it grows in rocky or alpine environments. The specific classification of Hieracium caloprasinum is subject to ongoing research and refinement in the field of botany.
uses: Hieracium caloprasinum is a lesser-known species, and as such, its medicinal uses are not well-documented.
